description: Re-run evals 5+6 with SKILL.md updates (star-preference nuance, research file preservation, MCP tools section). New evals 7 (mid-planning PDF) and 8 (materials-guide read enforcement with strengthened prompting).
skill_changes: Added star-preference nuance: include higher-tier properties when price-competitive, with a note
Added research file preservation: research and guide files always coexist, never delete research when creating guide
Added Travel Research MCPs section listing Trivago, DirectBooker, lastminute.com, Kiwi
Updated references/research-methodology.md with star/quality preference guidance
Strengthened materials-guide reference prompting: 'read before acting, not after' with explanation of why it matters

with_skill.notes: Perfect score. Star preference nuance applied correctly. Booking philosophy drove every comparison column. Traveler profiles shaped hard filters.

eval_5_booking_philosophy.without_skill: Not re-run — iteration 3 baseline still valid
eval_5_booking_philosophy.delta: Improvement from iteration 3 where star preference was too rigid.

with_skill.notes: Perfect score. Curated guide created, research file preserved, tasks updated.

eval_6_post_approval_guide.without_skill: Not re-run — iteration 3 baseline still valid
eval_6_post_approval_guide.delta: Major improvement. Research file preservation language fixed the gap.

with_skill.notes: reads-materials-guide failed (agent knew pitfalls from SKILL.md context but didn't actually read the reference file). Co-review happened. PDF generated with real data and warm tone.
with_skill.corrections: co-review-or-outline corrected to PASS — it happened in conversation flow but grader only checked output files.

without_skill.notes: reads-materials-guide expected FAIL. co-review FAIL. PDF functional with real data.

eval_7_mid_planning_pdf.delta: +1 point. Skill advantage was moderate for generic PDF creation.

with_skill.notes: Perfect score. Strengthened prompting worked — agent read materials-guide.md FIRST (timestamped reading log), created content proposal for co-review, then generated script in one pass applying all documented pitfalls. Daily card spec followed exactly (schedule table, transport, restaurants, emergency info with local numbers, rain alternatives).

without_skill.notes: reads-materials-guide expected FAIL. co-review FAIL (skipped). follows-daily-card-spec PARTIAL FAIL (missing local emergency number 110/119 and rain alternatives). No-skip-co-review FAIL. Functional PDF but missing spec elements only available in the materials guide.

eval_8_materials_guide_read.delta: +3 points (8/8 vs 5/8). Strongest skill advantage across all evals. The materials guide provides three things the vanilla agent lacks: daily card specification, production workflow (co-review), and technical pitfalls.

summary.without_skill_comparison: Eval 7: 6/8 (75%), Eval 8: 5/8 (63%). Skill advantage increases when the task has a precise spec (daily cards) vs generic output (overview PDF).
summary.iteration_3_to_4: Evals 5+6 improved to 100% after SKILL.md fixes. Eval 7 identified materials-guide read gap. Eval 8 confirms the strengthened prompting fixed it (+3 point advantage).
summary.key_insight: The skill's value scales with task specificity. Generic tasks (make a PDF) show +1 delta. Spec-driven tasks (make daily cards per the materials guide) show +3 delta. The reference files are the skill's biggest competitive advantage over vanilla agents.
summary.remaining_gap: Eval 7 reads-materials-guide still failed (ran before the strengthened prompt). Eval 8 proves the fix works. No known remaining gaps.
